reference: Sir, I am suffering from a fever for the past week. I had a blood test done the day before yesterday. The doctor says I am infected with leptospirosis and Salmonella Typhi O antigen - positive one:eighty, Salmonella Typhi H antigen - positive one:sixteen, Salmonella Paratyphi A H antigen - negative one:twenty, Salmonella Paratyphi B H antigen - negative one:twenty, Microscopic Agglutination Test - positive ++, and the Microscopic Agglutination Test for Leptospira Semaranga is one:eighty. Is it necessary for me to be hospitalized, or can I undergo treatment as an outpatient?
text: Sir, I am suffering from a fever for the past week. I had a blood test on the day before yesterday. The doctor says I am infected with leptospirosis and salmonella typhi O antigen positive 1 E ID salmonella typhi H antigen positive 1 16 salmonella paratyphi A H antigen negative 1 20 microscopic agglutination test for leptospira samarenga is 1 E. Is it necessary to be hospitalized or can I undergo treatment as an outpatient?
